In this episode of Tech Disruptors, they discuss how AI data centers are reshaping infrastructure finance, from hyperscaler-back

Ricardo Herrero, executive director of the Cuba Study Group, joins Bloomberg Intelligence’s Elliott Stein and Nathan Dean on this episode of the Votes and Verdicts podcast to discuss these and other related questions. Herrero also discusses the risks and opportunities for companies operating or seeking to operate in Cuba, a

title: Auto Industry Fears USMCA Shakeup

summary: Wall Street Host David Westin breaks down why the auto industry may want Washington to leave USMCA mostly intact. While Trump is focused on trade deficits and tougher rules, automakers are more worried about uncertainty, long-term investment and China’s growing role in North American supply chains. (Source: Bloomberg)

summary: Emerging-market stocks headed for their biggest weekly decline since early March, weighed down by a renewed selloff in technology shares that hammered South Korean equities.

title: Cantor Bitcoin SPAC Lets Investors Pledge Less as Crypto Slides

title: Boeing Wins Jet Deal From China Southern Valued at $3.62B
title_cn: 波音赢得南航喷气式飞机交易，价值 $3.62B
summary:
Boeing Co. secured an order from China Southern Airlines Co. valued at $3.62 billion, marking an important win for the US manufacturer after seeing orders dry up from Asia’s largest aviation market in the past decade.
George Ferguson, Bloomberg Intelligence Senior Aerospace, Defense, & Airlines Analyst, discusses the deal and what it means for Boeing. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Canadian Testing Company CSA Taps Jefferies to Explore a Sale

summary: CSA Group, the Canadian product testing and certification company, is exploring a sale that could value the business at around C$2 billion ($1.4 billion), according to people familiar with the matter.

title_cn: SpaceX 新债务损失持续扩大，令债券交易商震惊
summary: SpaceX’s blockbuster bond sale is weakening so quickly in the secondary market that traders say they can’t recall another recent deal that widened this sharply.

title: Stocks Edge Up at End of Bruising Week to AI Trade: Markets Wrap
title_cn: 股市在人工智能交易的痛苦周结束时小幅上涨：市场总结
summary: US stocks edged higher as dip buyers stepped in after a tumultuous week for chipmakers. Oil prices tumbled with more ships crossing through the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Wall Street Embraces the Dollar as Warsh’s Fed Activates Bulls
title_cn: 随着沃什的美联储激活多头，华尔街拥抱美元
summary: Only a year ago, Wall Street banks couldn’t come up with enough reasons to sell the dollar. Whether it was de-dollarization, “hedge America” or the debasement trade, the dollar was doomed.

title: SpaceX Index Era Sets Up Clash Among Shorts and Passive Billions
title_cn: SpaceX 指数时代引发空头和被动数十亿美元之间的冲突
summary: SpaceX’s stock has started to lose momentum after its blockbuster market debut. But investors betting against Elon Musk’s newest public company will soon face a different force: billions of dollars in mechanical buying as the stock enters major indexes.

title: Tallest Building in Beijing Damaged After Apparent Small Airplane Crash
title_cn: 北京最高建筑因小型飞机失事而受损
summary: Images and video appearing to show a small aircraft crashing into Beijing’s CITIC tower spread across Chinese social media on Friday. Bloomberg could not independently verify the images or circumstances of the incident at the city’s tallest building. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: New York Mets Fire Manager Mendoza After Rough First Half
title_cn: 纽约大都会队消防经理门多萨在上半场表现不佳后
summary:
The last-place New York Mets fired manager Carlos Mendoza and replaced him with Andy Green, an executive in the team’s front office, for the remainder of the season. 
Randall Williams, Bloomberg Business of Sports Reporter, discusses the move from the Mets as well as the World Cup so far. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Euro Zone Probably Saw First Inflation Slowdown Since Iran War Began
title_cn: 欧元区可能出现伊朗战争爆发以来首次通胀放缓
summary: The euro zone’s first signs of respite in inflation since the outbreak of the Iran war are forecast for data next week that will reflect the impact of subsiding energy costs.

title: Morgan Stanley, Goldman Shares Fall on Possible OpenAI IPO Delay
title_cn: 摩根士丹利和高盛股价因 OpenAI IPO 可能延迟而下跌
summary: Shares of Morgan Stanley and Goldman Sachs Group Inc. fell on Friday after OpenAI is said to weigh holding off on an initial public offering until next year amid volatility in technology stocks.

title: US Stocks Pare Drop on Improved Sentiment Despite Tech Declines
title_cn: 尽管科技股下跌，但由于市场情绪改善，美国股市跌幅收窄
summary: US stocks trimmed earlier losses Friday on a survey showing cooler-than-expected inflation expectations, easing rate hike concerns and helping offset weakness in chip stocks that began overnight.

title: US Oil Plunges Below $70 as Ships Keep Crossing Strait of Hormuz
title_cn: 由于船只不断穿越霍尔木兹海峡，美国石油价格跌破 70 美元
summary: Oil fell after transits through the Strait of Hormuz accelerated, with markets brushing off a Thursday attack on a cargo ship that renewed concerns about safe passage through the vital waterway.

title: Brazil’s Top Pork Exporter Boosting Output to Target Asia Demand
title_cn: 巴西最大猪肉出口国提高产量以满足亚洲需求
summary: Brazil’s largest pork exporter, cooperative Aurora, is expanding capacity as it bets on rising demand from Asia, underscoring the growing importance of the protein in global meat markets.

title: Bosch CEO Unexpectedly Leaves Biggest Car-Parts Maker
title_cn: 博世首席执行官意外离开最大汽车零部件制造商
summary: Robert Bosch GmbH Chief Executive Officer Stefan Hartung is stepping down after leading a push to cut thousands of jobs and make the world’s biggest automotive supplier more competitive.

title: US Consumer Sentiment Rises But Remains Near Record Low
title_cn: 美国消费者信心上升但仍接近历史低点
summary:
US consumer sentiment rose in June as lower gasoline prices provided some relief for Americans grappling with high inflation.
Lauren Saidel-Baker, Economist at ITR Economics, discusses the outlook for consumer spending and the path forward for the Fed as inflation concerns reign. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Rocket Lab Rises on NASA Selection; Microsoft Down on Tech Selloff | Stock Movers
title_cn: 火箭实验室因 NASA 选择而崛起；微软因科技股抛售而下跌库存搬运工
summary:
On this episode of Stock Movers with Alexis Christoforous:

- US chipmaker Onsemi (ON) has agreed to buy Synaptics (SYNA), a firm that specializes in semiconductors for smart devices, in an all-stock deal that values the company at about $6.2 billion. 
- Rocket Lab (RKLB) shares rise as the space firm says NASA selected it to provide three Electron launches for two missions, PolSIR and TSIS-2, from early 2027.
- Microsoft (MSFT) shares are down, with the software giant extending a recent selloff. The stock is down more than 20% this month, putting it on track for its biggest one-month percentage drop since December 2000 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: China Three Gorges Is Said In Talks to Buy German Wind Assets
title_cn: 据称中国三峡正在洽谈收购德国风电资产
summary: China Three Gorges Corp. is in talks to buy a German wind portfolio from NEAG Norddeutsche Energie AG, according to a person with knowledge of the matter.

title: Bed Bath & Beyond Nabs Bull on ‘Everything Home’ Transformation
title_cn: 床浴& Beyond Nabs Bull 谈“一切如家”转型
summary: Bed Bath & Beyond, Inc. gained a new bull this week who sees upside for shares on the premise that it will soon be known as a one-stop shop for home improvement and financing services as well as its pillows and pots.

title: Oil Heads for Weekly Decline Despite Hormuz Ship Attack
title_cn: 尽管霍尔木兹船只遭到袭击，石油价格仍将每周下跌
summary: Oil resumed its decline and headed for the biggest weekly drop in a month after transits through the Strait of Hormuz accelerated following an attack on a cargo ship. Tyler Kendall reports on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US Consumer Sentiment Rises But Remains Close to Record Low
title_cn: 美国消费者信心上升但仍接近历史低点
summary: US consumer sentiment rose in June as lower gasoline prices provided some relief for Americans grappling with high inflation.

title: US Natural Gas Rises on Hot Forecasts as July Contract Expires
title_cn: 7 月合同到期，美国天然气因热门预测而上涨
summary: US natural gas futures increased in the final day of trading for the July contract as forecasts continued to show above-average temperatures in the first week of July. Individual transactions moved the market more amid a drop-off in liquidity near expiry.

title: Saudi Arabia Is Ramping Up Oil Exports as Gulf Ports Restart
title_cn: 随着海湾港口重启，沙特阿拉伯正在增加石油出口
summary: Saudi Arabia is boosting crude shipments, opening up its Persian Gulf ports that were shuttered by the Iran war while also increasing loadings at Red Sea outlets that it has used to bypass the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Aluminum Heads for Fourth Weekly Drop on Mideast Supply Return
title_cn: 中东供应恢复，铝价将连续第四周下跌
summary: Aluminum headed for a fourth weekly decline, the longest losing streak since April 2025, as a renewed selloff in tech stocks added to pressure from the dollar’s recent rally and returning Middle East supplies.

title: Oil Prices Might Keep Falling Into 2027, Strategist Amoroso Says
title_cn: 策略师阿莫罗索表示，2027 年油价可能会持续下跌
summary: Anastasia Amoroso, chief investment strategist at Partners Group, says the concerns about higher oil prices did not play out. Speaking with Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Open Interest," Amoroso also says she expects energy prices to keep falling going into 2027. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Cost of the AI Boom: Anxiety, Longer Hours
title_cn: 人工智能繁荣的成本：焦虑、更长的工作时间
summary: One of the promises of AI was alleviating the burdens of work, but the workaholic culture in Silicon Valley hasn't disappeared. Bloomberg's Natasha Mascarenhas explains why round-the-clock managing of AI agents and intense competition are driving longer hours and heightened anxiety for tech workers. She joins Ed Ludlow on "Bloomberg Tech" (Source: Bloomberg)

title: European Stocks Fall on Renewed Tech Woes; Zalando Declines
title_cn: 欧洲股市因新一轮科技困境而下跌；扎兰多衰落
summary: European shares fell from record levels amid shifting sentiment toward artificial intelligence and technology stocks.

title: How Geothermal Energy Could Power the AI Boom
title_cn: 地热能如何推动人工智能的繁荣
summary: Can geothermal energy solve the AI energy crunch? I-Pulse Co-Founder, Chairman and CEO Robert Friedland explains how a $250 million US investment will accelerate breakthrough semiconductor technology designed to drill deep into Earth's heat, delivering around-the-clock clean energy. He joins Bloomberg Open Interest to make a  case for government-backed industrial policy and why geothermal, and not wind or solar, is the future of America's energy infrastructure. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: AI Cost Reality Check Hits Asia Tech Stocks as Apple Hikes Prices
title_cn: 随着苹果公司提高价格，人工智能成本现实检查冲击亚洲科技股
summary:
Asian technology stocks slumped after Apple Inc. and Microsoft Corp. raised prices for their products, stoking concern that rising component costs will curb demand for devices and eventually slow the memory chip rally that has powered much of the AI trade.
Bloomberg Tech Host Ed Ludlow discusses the move from Apple to raise prices as well as what's next for OpenAI as they mull a delay for the company's IPO. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Elroy Air’s Cargo Drone Bet
title_cn: Elroy Air 的货运无人机赌注
summary: Elroy Air is going public at a roughly $1 billion valuation, aiming to replace delivery trucks with long-range cargo drones. CEO Andrew Clare joins Bloomberg Open Interest to explain how the company’s heavy-payload aircraft works, why public markets are the right funding path and why defense demand and US supply-chain automation make this the ideal moment to scale. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: BlackBerry CEO Giamatteo Sees QNX Powering Growth
title_cn: 黑莓首席执行官 Giamatteo 认为 QNX 推动增长
summary: BlackBerry CEO John Giamatteo discusses the company’s evolution into a software and infrastructure player, centered on its QNX operating system. He highlights that QNX now powers over 275 million vehicles globally and is increasingly used in robotics, medical devices and industrial automation. Giamatteo speaks with Bloomberg's Dani Burger and Alpine Saxon Woods Chief Market Strategist Sarah Hunt on "Bloomberg Open Interest." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Apple’s Touch MacBook to Use M5 Pro and Max Chips, With M7 Models to Follow
title_cn: Apple 的 Touch MacBook 将使用 M5 Pro 和 Max 芯片，M7 型号也将跟进
summary: Apple Inc.’s first-ever touch-screen laptop will rely on the company’s current high-end M5 chips, rather than next-generation silicon, though the company is working on a follow-up model with future M7 processors.

title: The Cult of the C-Suite Is Officially Over
title_cn: 对最高管理层的崇拜正式结束
summary:
A decade ago, it seemed like chief executives were broadly admired if not universally beloved. But these days, general sentiment over top executives has significantly shifted. And on top of that, CEOs increasingly complain they are overworked, overstressed and subjected to endless scrutiny.

Bloomberg Opinion's Beth Kowitt joins Brad Stone and Max Chafkin on the Everybody's Business podcast to unpack why many top talents are turning down CEO jobs and how the culture shift may impact everyday workers. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Stocks Waver at End of Bruising Week for AI Trade
title_cn: 人工智能交易的挫败周结束时股市波动
summary:
Another burst of selling pummeled chipmakers, whipsawing stocks once again amid concerns over whether vast investments in artificial intelligence will justify the industry’s hefty valuations.
Virginie Maisonneuve, Founder at Maisonneuve Global Advisors, discusses the current AI narrative as investors continue to show jitters around high-flying tech stocks. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: World Bank, AfDB to Boost African Power Plan This Year
title_cn: 世界银行和非洲开发银行今年将推动非洲电力计划
summary: The World Bank and African Development Bank plan to accelerate their program to bring electricity to hundreds of millions of Africans this year by approving new projects, investing in Eritrea and promoting the development of regional power pools.

title: AI Is Already Reshaping US Politics at Every Level
title_cn: 人工智能已经在各个层面重塑美国政治
summary: From data center backlash to boundless cash from tech billionaires and concerns about deepfaked campaign ads, AI is everywhere in the 2026 US elections.

title: Market Underappreciates AI Capex Returns, Strategist Amoroso Says
title_cn: 策略师阿莫罗索表示，市场低估了人工智能资本支出回报
summary: Anastasia Amoroso, chief investment strategist at Partners Group, discusses the recent volatility in tech stocks with Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Open Interest." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: OHB Shares Dip After Satellite Firm’s €789 Million Re-IPO
title_cn: 卫星公司 7.89 亿欧元重新首次公开募股后 OHB 股价下跌
summary: Executives from German satellite firm OHB SE and KKR & Co. rang the opening bell at the Frankfurt Stock Exchange on Friday after they raised €789 million in a share sale to increase investors’ access to the space company.

title: Disney’s Parks Revamp Includes Aquatic Robots and Hovering Star Wars Droids
title_cn: 迪士尼公园改造包括水上机器人和悬停星球大战机器人
summary: Inside a Walt Disney Co. research and development lab, supported by some heavy-duty scaffolding, sits a wooden manta ray prototype roughly the size of a car. If all goes according to plan, the final version will be able to transform into the Gramma Tala character from Moana, used at a lagoon in a Disney park sometime soon. Down the line, the company will add a fleet of dolphin-like robots to the experience.

title: Inside Disney’s Push to Upgrade Its Theme Parks With Robots
title_cn: 迪士尼推动用机器人升级主题公园的内幕
summary: Inside Disney’s Push to Upgrade Its Theme Parks With Robots (Source: Bloomberg)

title: AI Trade’s Bruising Week Forces Investors to Be More Selective
title_cn: AI Trade 的惨痛一周迫使投资者更加挑剔
summary: This roller-coaster week for tech stocks from Seoul to New York fueled by extreme investor positioning and worries over chip demand is sending a strong signal: the case for the artificial-intelligence trade is still strong, but the days of everything going up in a straight line appear to be over.

title: Why the AI Boom Is Making Everything More Expensive
title_cn: 为什么人工智能的繁荣让一切变得更加昂贵
summary: AI demand is triggering a historic memory-chip shortage. Meeting exponential demand for chips will be expensive and maybe even impossible.

title: Ship Struck in Hormuz, Apple's Price Hikes Hit iPads & Macs | The Opening Trade 6/26/2026
title_cn: 霍尔木兹海难，苹果 iPad 和 Mac 涨价开盘交易 2026 年 6 月 26 日
summary:
A ship was hit by an unidentified projectile in the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday in the first reported attack since an interim US-Iran peace deal, marking a setback to efforts to restore traffic through the vital energy thoroughfare. 

Apple shares fell after it raised prices of all Macs, iPads, home devices and the Vision Pro on Thursday, seeking to offset cost hikes caused by an unprecedented shortage of memory chips and storage.

The Opening Trade has everything you need to know as markets open across Europe. With analysis you won't find anywhere else, we break down the biggest stories of the day and speak to top guests who have skin in the game. Hosted by Anna Edwards and Tom Mackenzie. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: JPMorgan's Kapnick on Europe Power Grid & Renewables Investment
title_cn: 摩根大通的卡普尼克谈欧洲电网和可再生能源投资
summary: The global economy will see $1 trillion of losses per year by 2050 if we don't adapt to changing climate patterns says Sarah Kapnick, Global Head of Climate Advisory at JPMorgan. She sees this leading to a 'structural investment trend' which will see more investment in the power grid and energy transition, especially geothermal and nuclear energy. She spoke to Bloomberg's Lizzy Burden on "The Pulse." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: OpenAI Makes Its Advertising Pitch at Cannes Festival
title_cn: OpenAI 在戛纳电影节上进行广告宣传
summary: The ChatGPT maker offers the industry a new marketing vehicle

title: SoftBank Shares Fall on Report OpenAI to Delay IPO
title_cn: OpenAI 推迟 IPO 消息导致软银股价下跌
summary: SoftBank Group’s stock tumbled the most since August 2024 after reports that OpenAI may hold off on an initial public offering until next year, delaying returns for its Japanese backer. Neil Campling reports on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Washington’s Foreign Ban on Anthropic’s Top Models May Backfire
title_cn: 华盛顿对 Anthropic 顶级模特的外国禁令可能会适得其反
summary: The move may act as a tailwind for rival China, boosting demand for the Asian nation’s open source AI.

title: VW Eyes as Many as 100,000 Job Cuts
title_cn: 大众预计裁员多达 10 万人
summary: Volkswagen is looking to cut tens of thousands of additional jobs and may shutter factories in a push by CEO Oliver Blume to make Europe’s biggest automaker more competitive, according to a report in Manager Magazin. Bloomberg's Elisabeth Behrmann has more. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: AI Cost Reality Check Hits Asia Tech Stocks as Apple Hikes Price
title_cn: 苹果提高股价，人工智能成本现实检验冲击亚洲科技股
summary: Asian technology stocks slumped after Apple Inc. and Microsoft Corp. raised prices for their products, stoking concern that rising component costs will curb demand for devices and eventually slow the memory chip rally that has powered much of the AI trade.

title: Goldman: Could Make Sense to Diversify Away From Chipmakers
title_cn: 高盛：摆脱芯片制造商的多元化可能是有意义的
summary: Christian Mueller-Glissmann, managing director for portfolio strategy and asset allocation at Goldman Sachs, discusses the tech selloff and why he thinks it could make sense to diversify away from semiconductor makers and lean more toward hyperscalers. "These are incredibly cyclical companies," Mueller-Glissmann tells Bloomberg Television. "So to some extent, it could make sense to diversify a bit away from them." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: GCash Owner Said to Seek $1.5 Billion in Record Philippine IPO
title_cn: GCash 所有者据称将寻求 15 亿美元融资，创菲律宾首次公开募股纪录
summary: The company behind the Philippines’ biggest mobile wallet GCash is preparing what may become the country’s biggest initial public offering ever, seeking to raise as much as 92.3 billion pesos ($1.5 billion).

title: Newsom Pitches Federal Wealth Tax After Losing California Battle
title_cn: 纽瑟姆在输掉加州之战后提出联邦财富税
summary: Governor Gavin Newsom is calling for a federal tax on billionaires after failing to stop a California wealth tax from reaching the November ballot, setting up a high-profile test of whether voters will back one of the nation’s most aggressive efforts to tax the rich.

title: Trump Says Iran Violated Ceasefire With Hormuz Drone Attack
title_cn: 特朗普称伊朗霍尔木兹无人机袭击违反停火协议
summary: US President Donald Trump accused Iran of violating its ceasefire agreement by firing at cargo ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz.

title: California to Vote on Raising Bar for Cities to Increase Taxes
title_cn: 加州将投票决定提高城市增税门槛
summary: California voters will decide in November whether to approve a measure that would make it significantly harder to hike local taxes through the ballot box.

title: Vance Says Nixon’s Watergate Wouldn’t End a Presidency Now
title_cn: 万斯表示尼克松的水门事件不会立即结束总统任期
summary: Vice President JD Vance called it “crazy” that the Watergate scandal toppled Richard Nixon’s presidency, saying it would have barely registered in today’s political environment.

title: Germany Weighs €20 Billion Tax Cut as Merz Seizes Momentum
title_cn: Merz 乘势而上，德国考虑减税 200 亿欧元
summary: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz’s coalition is negotiating as much as €20 billion ($22.8 billion) in income-tax relief as it taps momentum for its broader reform agenda, though the parties remain divided on how to pay for the cuts.

title: Oman Tells Allies Ships Going Through Hormuz May Have to Pay
title_cn: 阿曼告诉盟国，经过霍尔木兹海峡的船只可能必须付出代价
summary: Oman has told European officials there’s no way of going back to the pre-war status quo with the Strait of Hormuz and transiting ships may have to be charged some fees, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: UAE Residents Told to Ignore Missile Warning Minutes After Alert
title_cn: 阿联酋居民被告知在警报几分钟后忽略导弹警告
summary: Residents across the United Arab Emirates received emergency alerts on their phones on Friday warning of a potential missile threat, minutes before authorities issued an all-clear and instructed people to disregard the original message.

title: Johannesburg Struggles to Keep Lights on But Repays Bondholders
title_cn: 约翰内斯堡努力维持运转但偿还债券持有人
summary: The City of Johannesburg, which is on the verge of financial collapse, put investors at the front of its queue of creditors this week when it repaid a 1.44 billion rand ($88 million) bond.

title: Canada PM's Home Is Falling Apart and Carney Seeks Cash to Fix It
title_cn: 加拿大总理的家正在倒塌，卡尼寻求现金来修复它
summary: Canada is launching a fundraising campaign to restore its decaying, at times rodent-infested prime ministerial residence — repairs previous governments avoided for fear of backlash over the cost to taxpayers.

title: Venezuela Quake Crisis Tests Legitimacy of Rodriguez Regime
title_cn: 委内瑞拉地震危机考验罗德里格斯政权的合法性
summary: The earthquakes that struck Venezuela Wednesday have become the first major political test for acting President Delcy Rodríguez, with the disaster quickly evolving into a contest over competence.

title: Tanzania Bans Political Rallies, Bracing for Gen-Z Protests
title_cn: 坦桑尼亚禁止政治集会，为 Z 世代抗议做好准备
summary: Tanzania’s authorities banned political rallies and placed the country on high alert for youth-led protests against increased authoritarianism.

title: Chaotic Push to Train Staff for US Ebola Outbreak Facility Alarms Health Workers
title_cn: 美国埃博拉疫情设施培训工作人员的混乱推动给卫生工作者敲响了警钟
summary: The three-day session focused more on administrative tasks than spread prevention.

title: China’s State Media Say EU Risks ‘Freezing’ of Ties Over Trade
title_cn: 中国官方媒体称欧盟面临“冻结”贸易关系的风险
summary: Chinese state media accused European Union officials of a “lack of sincerity” in preparatory talks ahead of a meeting between top trade negotiators, and warned that relations between the two regions could be damaged.

title: Europe Must Pressure Putin More as Ukraine Attacks, Estonia Says
title_cn: 爱沙尼亚称，乌克兰袭击时欧洲必须向普京施加更多压力
summary: Estonia called for more “strategic patience” and increased pressure on Russia from European allies as President Vladimir Putin faces long-range attacks from Ukraine.

title: Ukraine’s Crimea Attacks Expose Limits of Putin’s Protection
title_cn: 乌克兰克里米亚袭击暴露了普京保护的局限性
summary: Vladimir Putin’s war in Ukraine is rebounding on Russia in Crimea, amid escalating attacks on the Black Sea peninsula that he annexed in 2014.

title: Morgan Stanley Upbeat on South African Assets Despite Headwinds
title_cn: 尽管面临阻力，摩根士丹利仍看好南非资产
summary: Morgan Stanley is positive on South African assets as headwinds from the Iran war fade, arguing that an approaching end to interest-rate hikes and further credit-rating upgrades will provide support.

title: Make Dulles Great Again: a $22 Billion Plan to Fix the Airport
title_cn: 让杜勒斯再次伟大：耗资 220 亿美元修复机场的计划
summary: A $22 billion proposal to renovate the DC-area airport in 8 years

title: UK’s Polanski Says Chancellor Streeting Would Be ‘Disastrous’
title_cn: 英国波兰斯基称财政大臣斯特里廷将是“灾难性的”
summary: Green Party leader Zack Polanski backed Ed Miliband over “disastrous” Wes Streeting to become the UK’s next chancellor in the latest sign of pressure building on Andy Burnham to name someone who will please the disaffected left.

title: Romanian President Has Two Choices for Premier to End Crisis
title_cn: 罗马尼亚总统有两种总理结束危机的选择
summary: Romania’s political establishment failed to break its deadlock as the four parties from the recently broken coalition offered competing candidates for the prime minister’s post.

title: Rats in the Chamber Show Johannesburg’s Decline
title_cn: 会议厅里的老鼠表明约翰内斯堡的衰落
summary: South Africa’s largest and most important city is “essentially bankrupt.”

title: Steve Hilton Says Trump isn't on California Ballot
title_cn: 史蒂夫·希尔顿表示特朗普不在加州选票上
summary:
Is Donald Trump a help or a hindrance in California?

Mishal Husain asks Republican candidate for governor Steve Hilton whether he wants the US president on the campaign trail. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Hormuz Traffic Still Flowing Despite Strike on Ship
title_cn: 尽管船舶罢工，霍尔木兹交通仍然畅通
summary: An attack on a container vessel sailing through the Strait of Hormuz has prompted some shipowners to review exit plans, but traffic continued to flow in both directions through the vital thoroughfare on Friday. Bloomberg’s Abeer Abu Omar reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Crypto Founder Delo Seals UK Return to Fund Farage War Chest
title_cn: 英国加密货币创始人 Delo 重返英国资助 Farage 战争基金
summary: Crypto entrepreneur Ben Delo finalized his return to the UK from Hong Kong, clearing the Reform donor’s path to bankroll Nigel Farage’s right-wing party as the nation cracks down on offshore political finance.

title: Zelenskyy Orders Push to End War as Russia Attacked by Drones
title_cn: 俄罗斯遭到无人机袭击，泽连斯基下令结束战争
summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy approved a 40-day “influence operation” to convince the Kremlin to end the war, as Russia said several regions were attacked in one of Kyiv’s most intense drone strikes in more than four years of war.

title: Odd Lots: Rory Johnston on Why Oil Didn’t Hit $200 (Podcast)
title_cn: 奇怪的事情：罗里约翰斯顿谈为什么石油没有达到 200 美元（播客）
summary: The Strait of Hormuz has (mostly) re-opened! Crude prices are still up since the start of the war with Iran, but popular predictions earlier this year of $200-a-barrel Brent didn’t pan out. Why is that? We last talked to Rory Johnston, the founder of the Commodity Context newsletter, at the start of the conflict. And in that conversation he said that the Strait’s closure would lead to $200 oil if it persisted for any length of time. Today, he returns to tell us what he’s learned about the oil ma

title: Burnham Inherits an Economy That’s Just Starting to Look Up
title_cn: 伯纳姆继承了一个刚刚开始好转的经济
summary: As Keir Starmer was reeling from heavy local election losses last month, it was hard to see why anyone would be trying to take over the embattled prime minister’s job.

title: US Envoy to Italy Praises Meloni, Downplays Trump Spat
title_cn: 美国驻意大利特使赞扬梅洛尼，淡化特朗普的口角
summary: The US ambassador to Italy moved to further ease tensions between the two allies, saying relations are “fine” in the aftermath of a public clash between Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni and President Donald Trump.

title: Bayer’s Supreme Court Win in Roundup Case No ‘Silver Bullet’
title_cn: 拜耳最高法院在农达案中获胜，没有“银弹”
summary: After years of legal setbacks that have cost billions of dollars over its top-selling Roundup weedkiller, Bayer AG finally got a resounding win in the biggest tribunal of all.
